page: Microsoft Azure HB368-192rs_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the HBv5 family. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 448.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.33 GB per core.

This server includes 15851 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, facilitating low-latency, high-throughput networking for clustered environments. The instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or onboard GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of dedicated compute cores combined with substantial local storage capacity positions this server for resource-intensive workloads. The low memory-per-core ratio of 2.33 GB represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-bound applications but highly efficient for compute-bound tasks that require fast local scratch space.

Appropriate workloads for this configuration include high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale parallel simulations, computational fluid dynamics, and database operations that leverage the massive local storage and RDMA capabilities.
description: Microsoft Azure HB368-192rs_v5 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the HBv5 family, featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is configured with 448.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.33 GB of memory per core, and includes 15851 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Designed with RDMA networking support, this server facilitates high-throughput, low-latency node communication. It does not feature integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The low memory-to-core ratio represents a design tradeoff optimized for raw computational density rather than memory-intensive datasets. This hardware profile is suited for high-performance computing, parallel simulations, and large-scale database workloads requiring high-capacity local storage and fast interconnects.
